name: Beowulf
description: Beowulf, a Geatish hero of immense strength and courage, is the central figure in the Old English epic poem of the same name. Born to Ecgtheow, a warrior of the Geats, and a daughter of King Hrethel, Beowulf's early life is shrouded in legend. He first rises to prominence by assisting King Hrothgar of Denmark, whose mead hall, Heorot, is under siege by the monstrous Grendel. Beowulf bravely confronts Grendel unarmed, tearing off the beast's arm and driving him back to his lair to die. Grendel's vengeful mother then attacks Heorot, and Beowulf follows her to her underwater cave, where he slays her with a giant's sword. Returning to Geatland, Beowulf eventually becomes king and rules wisely for fifty years. In his old age, he faces a final challenge: a fire-breathing dragon guarding a treasure hoard. Beowulf defeats the dragon, but suffers a mortal wound in the process. His death is mourned by his people, who build a barrow in his honor, ensuring his legend lives on. Beowulf represents the ideal of the heroic warrior: strong, loyal, brave, and willing to sacrifice himself for the good of his people. His story explores themes of courage, honor, fate, and the struggle between good and evil, reflecting the values of Anglo-Saxon society.
subtitle: The Hero of the Geats

keyMyths.title: The Battle with Grendel
keyMyths.description: Grendel terrorizes Heorot each night, until Beowulf arrives and fights him bare-handed. Beowulf tears off Grendel's arm, mortally wounding the monster.

keyMyths.title: The Fight with Grendel's Mother
keyMyths.description: To avenge her son, Grendel's Mother attacks Heorot. Beowulf follows her to her lair and defeats her with a magical sword.

keyMyths.title: Beowulf and the Dragon
keyMyths.description: As an old king, Beowulf battles a dragon guarding treasure. He defeats the dragon but is mortally wounded, proving his courage until the end.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: The primary source for the legend of Beowulf, an Old English epic poem.
sources.text: The primary source for the legend of Beowulf, an Old English epic poem.
